title: Sour Tender Algerian Dum Stir-Fry
description: This low-fat-friendly sour and tender dish features gelatine leafs, dum, and other essential ingredients for a sick-day meal in Algeria.
instructions: Preheat a wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the gelatine leafs and fry until they turn brown, about 3 minutes.
Remove the gelatine from the pan using tongs, ensuring it does not stick to the pan.
Drain the dum in hot water for a minute, then rinse with cold water. Pat dry with paper towels.
Add tomato sauce and red pepper flakes into the wok with the drained dum.
Stir-fry everything together until the sauce thickens and the dum is tender but not mushy, about 5 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course.
